name: Column Scan
summary: Find the shortest string length, then compare each character column across all strings until a mismatch stops the prefix.

lineExplanations.3: Initialize the answer as an empty string before any shared characters are confirmed.
lineExplanations.4: Save the first string because each compared character is taken from it as the reference.
lineExplanations.5: Use the first string length as the first limit for how far the prefix can extend.
lineExplanations.6: Loop through every string to find the shortest length among them.
lineExplanations.7: Load the string currently being considered for the shortest-length check.
lineExplanations.9: Check whether the current string is shorter than the current prefix limit.
lineExplanations.10: Shrink the allowed prefix length to the current shorter string length.
lineExplanations.11: Loop over each character position that could still be part of the prefix.
lineExplanations.12: Take the first string character at the current position as the reference character.
lineExplanations.13: Loop through the remaining strings to compare the same character position.
lineExplanations.14: Load the current string being compared against the first string.
lineExplanations.15: Read the current string's character at the active character position.
lineExplanations.16: Check whether this character differs from the first string's reference character.
lineExplanations.17: Store the prefix built before the mismatch as the final result.
lineExplanations.18: Return immediately because the first mismatch proves the prefix cannot grow further.
lineExplanations.19: Append the reference character because every string matched this column.
lineExplanations.20: Store the fully confirmed prefix after all possible positions were checked.
lineExplanations.21: Return the longest common prefix found by the column scan.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the wrapper class expected by LeetCode and the tracer.
syntaxExplanations.2: def declares the method; self is the object instance and strs is the input list.
syntaxExplanations.3: prefix = "" assigns an empty string to the variable prefix.
syntaxExplanations.4: strs[0] accesses the first element of the list.
syntaxExplanations.5: len(first) returns the number of characters in the first string.
syntaxExplanations.6: range(len(strs)) creates indexes from 0 through the last list index.
syntaxExplanations.7: s = strs[k] stores the string at index k in a shorter variable name.
syntaxExplanations.8: len(s) returns the number of characters in the current string.
syntaxExplanations.9: if runs the indented body only when current_len is smaller than min_len.
syntaxExplanations.10: min_len = current_len updates the saved shortest length.
syntaxExplanations.11: range(min_len) creates valid character indexes from 0 to min_len - 1.
syntaxExplanations.12: first[i] reads one character from the first string.
syntaxExplanations.13: range(1, len(strs)) creates indexes for all strings after the first.
syntaxExplanations.14: current = strs[j] stores the string at index j.
syntaxExplanations.15: current[i] reads the character at position i from the current string.
syntaxExplanations.16: != checks whether two values are different.
syntaxExplanations.17: result = prefix copies the current prefix into the result variable.
syntaxExplanations.18: return result exits the function and gives result back to the caller.
syntaxExplanations.19: prefix + ch creates a new string by adding ch to the end of prefix.
syntaxExplanations.20: result = prefix stores the completed prefix before returning it.
syntaxExplanations.21: return result exits the function with the final answer.
